finding: The Cloudflare Pages project identity is repeated across source configuration and deployment workflow inputs. A mismatch could direct deployment, metadata verification, or proof construction at different projects.

checks: Wrangler project name equals the canonical project identity
all workflow project references equal the canonical project identity
workflow project identity is present and unambiguous
Pages output directory remains the committed static root

verification.method: isolated Node test using byte-equivalent committed logic

limits: No Cloudflare credentials were available to this run.
The expected pages.dev hostname is derived from the committed project name and is not proof that DNS currently resolves.
The validator does not prove project existence, credential authorization, deployment success, or scientific truth.
falsification_route: Reject or revise the contract if Cloudflare returns a different canonical project identity for the deployed project, if the workflow can deploy a different project while the validator passes, or if the static output root legitimately changes.
